multipart

        items
      • items(mp: Multipart | MultipartRef): Boundary
      • items(ms: MultipartStreamer): Boundary
      • items(ms: MultipartStreamerRef): Boundary

This module implements a simple multipart/form-data parser for Nim.

It allows you to parse multipart form data from HTTP requests, handling both file uploads and text fields. The parser processes the multipart content, extracts file data and text fields, and provides a structured representation of the parsed data through the Multipart type.

The parser also supports callbacks for handling file data as it's being parsed, which can be useful for on-the-fly validation or processing of uploaded files.

Types

Boundary = object
  fieldName*: string
  case dataType*: MultipartDataType
  of MultipartFile:
    fileId*, fileName*, fileType*, filePath*: string
    fileContent*: File
    fileSize*: int64
    signatureState*: MultipartFileSigantureState
    magicNumbers*: seq[byte]
  else:
    value*: string
BoundaryState = enum
  boundaryInit, boundaryAdded, ## marks Boundary as added to `boundaries` sequenece
  boundaryRemoved ## can be set by external validators via `MultipartFileCallback`
                  ## once invalidated, the `Boundary` will be moved to `invalidBoundaries`
Multipart = object
  fileCallback*: MultipartFileCallback ## A `MultipartFileCallback` that runs while in
                                       ## `MultipartFile` boundary 
  fileSignatureCallback*: MultipartFileCallbackSignature ## Collects magic numbers while writing a file to disk
                                                         ## The callback must return one of 
                                                         ## `MultipartFileSigantureState` states. Use `stateMoreMagic`
                                                         ## to run `fileSignatureCallback` again for colelcting more bytes.
                                                         ## 
                                                         ## If the magic numbers are correct use `stateValidMagic`
                                                         ## to stop the callback and continue writing the file.
                                                         ## 
                                                         ## `stateInvalidMagic` will mark the boundary as invalid,
                                                         ## skip to `invalidBoundaries` and stops the callback.
                                                         ## 
                                                         ## `stateValidMagic` will continue writing the file on disk
                                                         ## and stops the signature callback
  progressCallback*: MultipartProgressCallback ## An optional `MultipartProgressCallback` that fires inline during parsing.
                                               ## Called for every progress event
  asyncProgressCallback*: MultipartAsyncProgressCallback ## An optional `MultipartAsyncProgressCallback` that fires inline during parsing.
                                                         ## Use this when you need to push progress events to an async stream (WebSocket, SSE)
                                                         ## without blocking the event loop.
  progressChunkInterval*: int64 ## How often (in bytes) to fire progressFileChunk.
                                ## Default is every 64KB, set to 0 to emit every byte (not recommended)
  invalidBoundaries*: seq[Boundary]
  sizeLimit*: MultipartSizeLimit
  fileWriteThreshold*: int
MultipartAsyncProgressCallback = proc (evt: MultipartProgress): Future[void] {.
    closure.}
async variant — for async callers (WebSocket, SSE etc.) 
MultipartDataType = enum
  MultipartFile, MultipartText
Types of multipart data 
MultipartFileCallback = proc (boundary: ptr Boundary; pos: int; c: ptr char): bool {.
    closure.}
A callback that runs while parsing a MultipartFile boundary 
MultipartFileCallbackSignature = proc (boundary: ptr Boundary; pos: int;
                                       c: ptr char): MultipartFileSigantureState {.
    closure.}
A callback that runs while parsing a MultipartFile boundary to collect and validate magic 
MultipartFileSigantureState = enum
  stateInvalidMagic, stateMoreMagic, stateValidMagic
States for file magic-number signature validation 
MultipartHeader = enum
  contentDisposition = "content-disposition", contentType = "content-type"
Supported multipart headers 
MultipartHeaderTuple = tuple[key: MultipartHeader, value: seq[(string, string)]]
A tuple representing a multipart header 
MultipartInvalidHeader = object of CatchableError
MultipartProgress = object
  kind*: MultipartProgressKind
  fieldName*: string         ## which field this event belongs to
  fileName*: string          ## empty for text fields / body events
  bytesWritten*: int64       ## running total for the current file
  totalBytes*: int64         ## body size (set on progressBodyStart/Done)
MultipartProgressCallback = proc (evt: MultipartProgress) {.closure.}
sync variant — for non-async callers, fired every progressChunkInterval bytes 
MultipartProgressKind = enum
  progressBodyStart,        ## fired once before parsing begins
  progressFileStart,        ## fired when a new file boundary opens
  progressFileChunk,        ## fired for every byte written to a file
  progressFileDone,         ## fired when a file boundary closes
  progressBodyDone           ## fired once after parsing completes
MultipartRef = ref Multipart
Ref-counted wrapper for Multipart. Required for parseAsync since the async macro captures mp into a closure state machine and var params cannot be captured. 
MultipartSizeLimit = object
  maxFileSize*: int64        ## Maximum size for a single file upload in bytes (0 = unlimited)
  maxBodySize*: int64        ## Maximum total body size in bytes (0 = unlimited)
  maxFieldSize*: int64       ## Maximum size for a single text field value in bytes (0 = unlimited)
Configurable size limits for multipart parsing 
MultipartSizeLimitError = object of CatchableError
Raised when a size limit is exceeded 
MultipartStreamer = object

Incremental multipart/form-data parser. Feed body data as it arrives from the network. No need to buffer the entire body.

Usage: var ms = newMultipartStreamer(contentType) ms.feed(chunk1) ms.feed(chunk2) ... if ms.isComplete(): for b in ms.boundaries(): ... ms.cleanup()

MultipartStreamerRef = ref MultipartStreamer
Ref-counted wrapper for MultipartStreamer. Required when the streamer must be captured in closures (e.g. onBodyData callbacks). 
MultipartTextCallback = proc (boundary: ptr Boundary; data: ptr string): bool {.
    closure.}

Procs

proc boundaries(ms: MultipartStreamer): lent seq[Boundary] {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc boundaries(ms: MultipartStreamerRef): lent seq[Boundary] {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc cleanup(mp: var Multipart | MultipartRef)
Removes all temporary files written to disk during parsing. Call this after you are done processing the boundaries. 
proc cleanup(ms: MultipartStreamerRef) {....raises: [], tags: [WriteDirEffect],
    forbids: [].}
proc cleanup(ms: var MultipartStreamer) {....raises: [], tags: [WriteDirEffect],
    forbids: [].}
Remove all temporary files written to disk during streaming parsing. 
proc cleanupInvalid(mp: var Multipart | MultipartRef)
Removes only the temporary files for boundaries that were rejected (invalid signature / callback rejection). 
proc cleanupInvalid(ms: MultipartStreamerRef) {....raises: [],
    tags: [WriteDirEffect], forbids: [].}
proc cleanupInvalid(ms: var MultipartStreamer) {....raises: [],
    tags: [WriteDirEffect], forbids: [].}
proc feed(ms: var MultipartStreamer; data: openArray[byte]) {.
    ...raises: [Exception, MultipartSizeLimitError, ValueError, IOError, OSError],
    tags: [RootEffect, TimeEffect, WriteIOEffect, WriteDirEffect], forbids: [].}
Feed a chunk of multipart body data. Can be called incrementally as data arrives from the network. 
proc feed(ms: var MultipartStreamer; data: ptr UncheckedArray[byte];
          dataLen: int) {....raises: [Exception, MultipartSizeLimitError,
                                   ValueError, IOError, OSError], tags: [
    RootEffect, TimeEffect, WriteIOEffect, WriteDirEffect], forbids: [].}
Zero-copy feed from a raw byte pointer. 
proc feed(ms: var MultipartStreamer; data: string) {.
    ...raises: [Exception, MultipartSizeLimitError, ValueError, IOError, OSError],
    tags: [RootEffect, TimeEffect, WriteIOEffect, WriteDirEffect], forbids: [].}
Convenience overload for feeding string data. 
proc getMagicNumbers(boundary: Boundary): lent seq[byte] {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Returns the magic numbers collected while parsing the boundary 
proc getPath(boundary: Boundary): lent string {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Return the file path of a Boundary object if the boundary data type is MultipartDataType Check type using getType 
proc getTempDir(mp: Multipart | MultipartRef): lent string
Returns the temporary directory path 
proc getTempDir(ms: MultipartStreamer): lent string {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
proc initMultipart(contentType: string;
                   fileCallback: MultipartFileCallback = nil;
                   progressCallback: MultipartProgressCallback = nil;
                   sizeLimit = MultipartSizeLimit(); tmpDir = ""): Multipart {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [ReadEnvEffect, ReadIOEffect], forbids: [].}
Initializes an instance of Multipart 
proc initMultipartRef(contentType: string;
                      fileCallback: MultipartFileCallback = nil;
                      progressCallback: MultipartProgressCallback = nil;
                      sizeLimit = MultipartSizeLimit(); tmpDir = ""): MultipartRef {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [ReadEnvEffect, ReadIOEffect], forbids: [].}
Ref variant of initMultipart. Use when you need parseAsync. 
proc invalidBoundaries(ms: MultipartStreamer): lent seq[Boundary] {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc invalidBoundaries(ms: MultipartStreamerRef): lent seq[Boundary] {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc isComplete(ms: MultipartStreamer): bool {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Returns true when the closing --boundary-- has been seen. 
proc isComplete(ms: MultipartStreamerRef): bool {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
proc len(mp: Multipart | MultipartRef): int
Returns the number of valid boundaries 
proc len(ms: MultipartStreamer): int {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc len(ms: MultipartStreamerRef): int {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc newMultipartStreamer(contentType: string;
                          fileCallback: MultipartFileCallback = nil;
    fileSignatureCallback: MultipartFileCallbackSignature = nil;
                          progressCallback: MultipartProgressCallback = nil;
                          sizeLimit = MultipartSizeLimit(); tmpDir = "";
                          bodySize = 0'i64): MultipartStreamer {.
    ...raises: [OSError, IOError],
    tags: [ReadEnvEffect, ReadIOEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadDirEffect],
    forbids: [].}
Create a new streaming multipart parser. contentType is the full Content-Type header value (e.g. "multipart/form-data; boundary=----WebKitFormBoundaryXYZ"). bodySize is the total body size if known (e.g. from Content-Length). 
proc newMultipartStreamerRef(contentType: string;
                             fileCallback: MultipartFileCallback = nil;
    fileSignatureCallback: MultipartFileCallbackSignature = nil;
                             progressCallback: MultipartProgressCallback = nil;
                             sizeLimit = MultipartSizeLimit(); tmpDir = "";
                             bodySize = 0'i64): MultipartStreamerRef {.
    ...raises: [OSError, IOError],
    tags: [ReadEnvEffect, ReadIOEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadDirEffect],
    forbids: [].}
proc parse(mp: var Multipart; body: seq[byte]; tmpDir = "") {.
    ...raises: [MultipartSizeLimitError, Exception, OSError, IOError, ValueError], tags: [
    RootEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteIOEffect, TimeEffect],
    forbids: [].}
Parse a Multipart instance synchronously from a multipart/form-data body provided as raw bytes. Avoids the overhead of converting from a string. 
proc parse(mp: var Multipart; body: string; tmpDir = "") {.
    ...raises: [MultipartSizeLimitError, Exception, OSError, IOError, ValueError], tags: [
    RootEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteIOEffect, TimeEffect],
    forbids: [].}

Parse and return a Multipart instance synchronously from a multipart/form-data body string.

Raises MultipartSizeLimitError if any of the specified size limits are exceeded during parsing.

proc parse(mp: var Multipart; data: ptr UncheckedArray[byte]; dataLen: int;
           tmpDir = "") {....raises: [MultipartSizeLimitError, Exception, OSError,
                                   IOError, ValueError], tags: [RootEffect,
    WriteDirEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteIOEffect, TimeEffect], forbids: [].}
Zero-copy parse from a raw byte pointer. The pointer must remain valid for the duration of parsing (e.g. points into the HTTP parser buffer during a request handler). 
proc parseAsync(mp: MultipartRef; body: seq[byte]; tmpDir = ""): owned(
    Future[void]) {....stackTrace: false, raises: [Exception,
    MultipartSizeLimitError, ValueError, OSError, IOError], tags: [RootEffect,
    WriteDirEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteIOEffect, TimeEffect], forbids: [].}
Async variant of parse for raw bytes input. 
proc parseAsync(mp: MultipartRef; body: string; tmpDir = ""): owned(
    Future[void]) {....stackTrace: false, raises: [Exception,
    MultipartSizeLimitError, ValueError, OSError, IOError], tags: [RootEffect,
    WriteDirEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteIOEffect, TimeEffect], forbids: [].}

Async variant of parse. Use when you need to push progress to a WebSocket or SSE stream without blocking the event loop.

Example:

mp.asyncProgressCallback = proc(evt: MultipartProgress): Future[void] {.async.} =
  await ws.send($evt)   # push to WebSocket

mp.progressChunkInterval = 64 * 1024  # emit every 64KB, not every byte
await mp.parseAsync(body)

proc parseAsync(mp: MultipartRef; data: ptr UncheckedArray[byte]; dataLen: int;
                tmpDir = ""): owned(Future[void]) {....stackTrace: false,
    raises: [Exception, MultipartSizeLimitError, ValueError, OSError, IOError], tags: [
    RootEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteIOEffect, TimeEffect],
    forbids: [].}
Async zero-copy variant of parse from a raw byte pointer.

Iterators

iterator items(mp: Multipart | MultipartRef): Boundary
Iterate over available boundaries in the Multipart instance

    forbids: [].}
iterator items(ms: MultipartStreamerRef): Boundary {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
